name: Immersive Stage
description: Full-bleed public shell for cinematic launch, game, venue, and live-event homepages where the first viewport is a designed stage rather than a standard stacked marketing page.

guidance.header_treatment: Header sits flush to the top edge and spans the full viewport width. Keep content constrained inside the header, but let the bottom accent line span edge-to-edge.
guidance.accent_line: Use a one-pixel border-gradient from cyan on the left through transparent middle into red or brand-hot accent on the right. The line belongs to the viewport, not the content container.
guidance.body_spacing: The page body owns its first-viewport composition. Do not apply default marketing shell section padding to the hero stage.
guidance.navigation: Use compact brand, two or three nav links, and one aggressive CTA. Avoid utility clutter in the public hero shell.
guidance.footer: No persistent footer is required for a staged homepage. If legal links are needed, place them after the cinematic content rather than inside the first viewport.

header.background: linear-gradient(180deg, color-mix(in srgb, var(--d-bg) 94%, black), transparent)
header.accent_line: absolute bottom edge, left:0, right:0, height:1px, linear-gradient(90deg, cyan, transparent 34%, rgba(255,255,255,.16), transparent 72%, hot accent)
header.center_content: Two or three nav anchors only
header.right_content: Primary sign-in or live action CTA
header.button_sizing: Header CTAs are compact, slanted or clipped when the theme supports it, and about 40px tall.

body.note: Route components render full-bleed sections directly. Do not wrap the body in a centered card or default d-section container.

code.imports: Use the target router outlet and compact nav/link primitives available in the app.
code.example: ImmersiveStageShell -> FixedHeader(edge-to-edge accent line) + MainOutlet(full-bleed body)
